params.text:
How care is regulated: the CQC

In England, adult social care is regulated by the Care Quality Commission (CQC). The CQC is the independent regulator that checks whether care services are safe, effective, caring, responsive and well-led. Providers of regulated care must register with the CQC and meet the fundamental standards — the standards below which care must never fall.

The fundamental standards include person-centred care, dignity and respect, consent, safety, safeguarding, good governance, having enough suitably skilled staff, and a duty of candour (being open and honest, especially when something goes wrong). The CQC inspects services, publishes ratings, and can take action where care is not good enough.

Understanding that you work within a regulated sector helps you see why agreed ways of working, records and the 6 Cs matter so much: they are how good, safe care is delivered and demonstrated every single day.

Bringing it together

You have now covered the foundations of working in adult social care:

  • What adult social care is and the many settings it happens in
  • Your role, responsibilities and limits as a care worker
  • The 6 Cs and the values and behaviours expected of you
  • Agreed ways of working and following policies and procedures
  • Working relationships, boundaries and team working
  • Person-centred care, duty of care, rights and confidentiality
  • Your own development, supervision and the Care Certificate
  • How the sector is regulated by the CQC

Keep these principles in mind as you continue your induction — they are the foundation of everything else you will learn.

params.question: Which organisation is the independent regulator of adult social care services in England?

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. The CQC registers, inspects and rates care services against the fundamental standards.
No. The NHS provides healthcare; it is not the regulator of social care services.
No. This has no role in regulating the quality and safety of care services.
No. Skills for Care supports workforce development and standards, but it is not the regulator — that is the CQC.
